name: Russell's Paradox
guide_hints: In this level, we will prove that there is no 'set of all sets', also known as Russell's paradox.
The goal is to prove that there does not exist a set A such that for all B, B belongs to A. The goal formula is `‚ąĄA ‚ąÄB (B‚ąąA)`.
First, we will expand our definition of ‚ąĄ. Type `rw "nexists" goal`.
Now we will assume the negation of our goal using the contra rule. Type `contra goal ant1 f1`. This assumes `‚ąÉA ‚ąÄB (B‚ąąA)` (let's call the premise ant1) and changes our goal to ‚ä• (f1).
Next, we want to instantiate this universal set A. First let's claim the assumption by typing `cf P ‚ąÉA ‚ąÄB (B‚ąąA)` followed by `apply P PC1 ant1`.
Now instantiate the existential quantifier to a set U: `intro P U`. Now P asserts `‚ąÄB (B‚ąąU)`.
Now we use the Axiom of Specification to define a set R of all sets that do not contain themselves.
Using the specification axiom, we know such a set exists. Type `apply thm1 specification` to prove it.
Next, we instantiate B to R. Type `intro thm1 R`. This yields `‚ąÄx (x‚ąąR ‚áĒ (x‚ąąU ‚ąß ¬¨(x‚ąąx)))`.
Now we instantiate x to R. Type `intro thm1 R`. This yields `R‚ąąR ‚áĒ (R‚ąąU ‚ąß ¬¨(R‚ąąR))`.
We also know R‚ąąU because U is the universal set. Type `intro P R` to yield `R‚ąąU`.
Now you have `R‚ąąR ‚áĒ (R‚ąąU ‚ąß ¬¨(R‚ąąR))` (thm1) and `R‚ąąU` (P). These logically imply ‚ä• (f1).
Type `apply f1 PC1 thm1 P` to deduce ‚ä• from these premises, resolving the contradiction and completing the mission!
Finally, type `exit` to close the mission environment!
